book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And after the letter was read, Cyril, the bishop of Alexandria, said: This holy and great Synod has heard what I wrote to the most religious Nestorius, defending the right faith.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: I think that I have in no respect departed from the true statement of the faith, that is from the creed set forth by the holy and great synod formerly assembled at Nice.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Wherefore I desire your holiness [i.e. the Council] to say whether rightly and blamelessly and in accordance with that holy synod I have written these things or no.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And all the rest of the bishops in the order of their rank deposed to the same things, and so believed, according as the Fathers had set forth, and as the Epistle of the most holy Archbishop Cyril to Nestorius the bishop declared.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Palladius, the bishop of Amasea, said, The next thing to be done is to read the letter of the most reverend Nestorius, of which the most religious presbyter Peter made mention; so that we may understand whether or no it agrees with the exposition of the Nicene fathers.…

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: And after this letter was read, Cyril, the bishop of Alexandria, said, What seems good to this holy and great synod with regard to the letter just read?

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Does it also seem to be consonant to the faith set forth by the holy Synod assembled in the city of Nice?

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: All the bishops cried out together: Whoever does not anathematize Nestorius let him be anathema.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Such an one the right faith anathematizes; such an one the holy Synod anathematizes.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Whoever communicates with Nestorius let him be anathema!

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: We anathematize all the apostles of Nestorius: we all anathematize Nestorius as a heretic: let all such as communicate with Nestorius be anathema, etc., etc.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Juvenal, the bishop of Jerusalem said: Let the letter of the most holy and reverend Cælestine, archbishop of the Church of Rome, be read, which he wrote concerning the faith.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Peter the presbyter of Alexandria, and primicerius of the notaries said: Altogether in agreement with the things just read are those which his holiness Cyril our most pious bishop wrote, which I now have at hand, and will read if your piety so shall order.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
verses.text: Τοῦ Σωτῆρος ἡμῶν λέγοντος ἐναργῶς, κ.τ.λ.

verses.book: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
